Biography

I am a master’s student in Mechanical Engineering at Johns Hopkins University. My work combines computational fluid dynamics, biofluid mechanics, and scientific computing in the Flow Physics and Computational Lab, where I am advised by Dr. Rajat Mittal and Dr. Jung-Hee Seo.

My current research focuses on pancreatic duct flow and the development of integrated computational tools to help identify chronic pancreatitis associated with pancreatic ductal hypertension. The work connects MRI-based reconstruction, geometric analysis, CFD simulation, pressure prediction, and physics-informed learning.

Before Johns Hopkins, I completed my undergraduate studies at Southern Illinois University and Shenyang Aerospace University, specializing in Aircraft Powerplant Engineering. My training covered fluid dynamics, energy systems, mechanical design, and hands-on propulsion engine assembly and maintenance.
